title: Making Directories: `mkdir`
description: Build the folders that will house your digital empire.

slides.title: Building Rooms: `mkdir` 📁
slides.content:
# Make Directory

Files are great, but they need a place to live. You can't have a clean system with 10,000 files in one folder.

The command is `mkdir` (Make Directory).

**Usage:**
```bash
mkdir new_folder_name
```

This creates an empty folder (directory) in your current location.

### Analogy: The Construction Crew 👷
`touch` is like placing a single brick. `mkdir` is like building a new empty room to put bricks in.

slides.title: Create a Project Folder
slides.content: Let's create a folder named `Hacking_Tools` and then move into it.
slides.codeSnippet:
mkdir Hacking_Tools
ls -l
cd Hacking_Tools
pwd

quizData.question: The command to create a new directory named 'Evidence' is `_____ Evidence`.

slides.title: Nested Directories: The `-p` Flag 🏗️
slides.content:
# Creating a Path of Folders

What if you want to create a folder inside a folder that doesn't exist yet?

```bash
mkdir Projects/2024/January
```

If `Projects` doesn't exist, this command will **FAIL** with an error: `No such file or directory`.

### The Solution: `-p` (Parents)
The `-p` flag tells `mkdir`: "Create any missing parent directories along the way."

```bash
mkdir -p Projects/2024/January
```

This creates:
1.  `Projects/`
2.  `Projects/2024/`
3.  `Projects/2024/January/`

All in one shot. This is essential for setting up complex project structures quickly.

slides.title: Try Nested Creation
slides.content: Create a deep folder structure for a penetration test report.

slides.title: Pro Tip 🚀
slides.content:
## `mkdir` and Spaces

Remember our lesson on spaces? If you want a folder named `My Projects`, you must:

```bash
mkdir "My Projects"
# OR
mkdir My\ Projects
```

Avoid spaces in folder names whenever possible. Use **underscores** or **hyphens** instead: `My_Projects` or `My-Projects`. It makes scripting much easier.

quizData.question: You want to create `/home/user/A/B/C` but `A` doesn't exist yet. Which command works?
quizData.explanation: The `-p` flag (parents) creates all necessary intermediate directories.
